description_long: This is an ornate porcelain vase with a flared, scalloped rim and a bulbous body tapering to a narrow base. The front features a detailed painted scene depicting figures in a pastoral setting, likely inspired by Rococo or Neoclassical art. Two elaborate, twisted handles in a reddish-brown color extend from the neck to the body, adding both decorative and functional elements. The vase is predominantly a pale blue-green with gold accents and a thin gold line tracing the rim and base. The back is plain, highlighting the contrast between the decorative front and the simpler rear. The surface appears glossy and smooth, suggesting a glazed ceramic finish.
description: An ornate, pale blue-green porcelain vase with twisted reddish handles and a painted pastoral scene on the front. Features a scalloped rim, gold accents, and a glossy finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the painted pastoral scene, both twisted handles, and the scalloped rim.
description_view_1: Side view revealing one handle in profile and the plain blue-green surface with a hint of the painted scene.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain blue-green back and both handles, with no painted decoration.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other handle in profile and a partial glimpse of the painted scene.
